title: Aureline graph provenance-stamp and source-anchor-drift contract
description: Boundary schema for the per-record provenance stamp and the typed source-anchor-drift vocabulary every graph-backed surface (graph overlay, topology map, impact explorer, cited-explainer overlay, AI context assembler, review pack, support bundle, public query-family surface) reads when it explains why an object or relation exists, how stale or inferred it is, and whether the source anchor still resolves. This schema is a strict superset of the inline provenance_stamp slot defined on schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json: every workspace-graph node and edge already carries a provenance_stamp from the seed; this contract adds the typed extraction_or_inference_mode, anchor_validity, source_anchor_drift_state, imported_root_status, and supporting_artifact_refs fields that downstream surfaces need to render typed certainty without inventing local-only confidence labels. Raw source bodies, raw URLs, raw provider URLs, and raw credentials never cross this boundary; every ref is an opaque id. Adding a new source class, new extraction-mode, new anchor-validity state, new drift state, new imported-root status, or new supporting-artifact class is additive-minor and bumps provenance_stamp_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

provenance_stamp_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the provenance-stamp / source-anchor-drift vocabulary. Current value is 1. Bumped on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape.

monotonic_timestamp.description: Producer-local monotonic timestamp. Opaque equality-comparable string. Prototype fixtures emit 'mono:...' tokens; production readers treat the value as opaque.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles. Never carries raw source bodies or raw URLs.

source_class.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#source_class. Names which producer family emitted the record. This contract does not mint new source classes.

provenance_class.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#provenance_class. Names how the current record came to carry this data.

extraction_or_inference_mode.description: How this record's payload was produced. Orthogonal to source_class (which producer family) and provenance_class (how the record carries the data); extraction_or_inference_mode names the path the producer took. observed_direct_extraction: producer observed the value first-hand (parser, symbol resolver, filesystem walker, build toolchain run). parsed_from_structured_source: extracted from a structured artifact (lockfile, OpenAPI doc, manifest). projected_from_authoritative_index: read from a downstream index that re-exports an authoritative producer. replayed_from_capture: rehydrated from a deterministic replay capture. hydrated_from_imported_bundle: hydrated from a signed upstream bundle, vendor drop, or docs-pack import. derived_by_heuristic: derived by a non-AI heuristic pass (cross-reference, fuzzy match, transitive walk). generated_by_codegen: produced by a deterministic codegen tool whose lineage is available. suggested_by_ai_inference: produced by an AI inference pass and carries the lower default confidence; surfaces never silently upgrade. manually_annotated: a human added or amended this record (triage tag, override note, manual review) and the body is redaction-aware.

freshness.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#freshness. Frames with non-authoritative freshness MUST carry a stale_reason.

stale_reason.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#stale_reason. null only when freshness == authoritative.

anchor_kind.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#source_anchor.anchor_kind. Names what the anchor points at.

anchor_validity_state.description: Three-state validity vocabulary every consumer reads when it decides whether to render a citation row, a drift chip, or a missing-anchor badge. anchor_present_resolves: anchor still points at the same object the producer originally observed. anchor_resolves_with_drift: anchor still resolves but the underlying object has changed (move, rename via alias, regenerated artifact, imported-bundle re-pin); the drift_state names which class of drift. anchor_unresolvable: the anchor no longer resolves and the surface MUST render the missing-anchor badge with the typed drift_state instead of collapsing into a generic 'not available' message.

source_anchor_drift_state.description: Frozen source-anchor drift vocabulary. Every record with a source_anchor names exactly one drift_state so topology, impact, and explainer surfaces render typed drift instead of generic 'not available' messaging. anchor_present_no_drift: pairs with anchor_validity_state == anchor_present_resolves. anchor_moved_resolves_via_alias: source moved but resolves through ADR-0006 alias_set or a forwarding rename; surfaces render a moved-anchor chip and quote the canonical_filesystem_object the resolver landed on. anchor_deleted_no_replacement: source object was deleted and no forwarding alias resolves; pairs with a missing_anchor_node and missing_reason in {deleted_filesystem_object, unresolved_symbol, provider_resource_unreachable}. anchor_imported_unverified: source_anchor lives inside an imported root and has not been re-validated against a workspace-local cross-check; pairs with provenance_class == imported_external and freshness == imported. anchor_generated_pending_lineage: source_anchor points at a generated artifact whose generated_artifact_lineage_record is missing or pending regeneration; pairs with drift_state == unknown_lineage on the lineage record. anchor_unknown_lineage: source_anchor lineage cannot be reconstructed (replay-capture missing entry, imported bundle missing entry, ai_inference without supporting evidence); surfaces render the unknown-lineage badge rather than hiding the row.

imported_root_status.description: Frozen imported-root vocabulary mirroring schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#node_body_imported_root.import_kind plus a not_imported sentinel. Every provenance stamp names exactly one value so consumers can render the imported-root chip without re-deriving the import_kind from the underlying imported_root_node. Records emitted by an authoritative local producer carry not_imported.

supporting_artifact_class.description: Vocabulary for the artifact families a provenance stamp may cite as supporting evidence. Each supporting_artifact_ref pairs an opaque ref with one class so support bundles, AI evidence packets, and review packs reuse the same vocabulary rather than minting per-surface synonyms.

note.description: Redaction-aware short note (≤ 240 graphemes). Surfaces render this verbatim. Never embeds raw source bodies, raw URLs, or raw credentials.

supporting_artifact_ref.description: One supporting-artifact reference. A provenance stamp may list any number; downstream surfaces walk this list to assemble a forensic packet without re-querying upstream producers.

last_known_ref.description: Optional opaque ref to the last-known identity of the anchor (previous canonical_filesystem_object, previous symbol qualified_path, previous provider resource_handle). Surfaces render this verbatim alongside the drift chip.

resolved_ref.description: Optional opaque ref the anchor resolves to under the current drift state (e.g. the alias the moved anchor lands on). Null when anchor_validity_state == anchor_unresolvable.

line_range.description: Optional line-range hint ('line_start:line_end'). Raw source bytes never appear here.

drift_note.description: Redaction-aware short note (≤ 240 graphemes) the surface renders verbatim alongside the drift chip.

anchor_drift_observation.description: One per-anchor drift observation. Every record's source_anchors list pairs each anchor with one drift observation so consumers render typed drift (moved / deleted / imported / generated / unknown) rather than collapsing into a generic 'not available' message.

stamp_id.description: Stable provenance-stamp id. Surfaces that quote the stamp (audit packets, support bundles, AI evidence packets) reuse this id rather than re-stating the contents.

subject_kind.description: Which surface family this stamp applies to. Every workspace-graph node and edge MUST carry exactly one stamp; impact-reason and explainer-citation slots MAY carry their own stamp when they project a different producer than the parent record.

subject_ref.description: Opaque ref to the subject node_id, edge_id, impact_reason_slot id, or explainer_citation_slot id.

workspace_id.description: Optional workspace identifier so a multi-workspace audit packet can group stamps.

anchor_drift_observations.description: One drift observation per source_anchor on the parent record. Empty only for synthetic subjects (missing_anchor_node, policy_view_node) that legitimately carry no source anchors.

supporting_artifact_refs.description: Zero or more supporting-artifact references. Support bundles and AI evidence packets walk this list to assemble a forensic packet.

producer_ref.description: Optional opaque producer ref (crate id, tool id, provider id, ai_provider id). Support bundles cite this verbatim.

producer_version.description: Optional producer version. A version change contributes to a later invalidation frame.

imported_bundle_ref.description: Opaque ref to the imported-bundle entry, when imported_root_status != not_imported.

replay_capture_ref.description: Opaque ref to the replay-capture entry, when provenance_class == replayed_capture.

support_ref.description: Optional opaque support-bundle anchor carrying the full forensic packet for this record.

stamp_note.description: Redaction-aware short note (≤ 240 graphemes) the surface renders verbatim. Never embeds raw source bodies, raw provider URLs, or raw credentials.

provenance_stamp_record.description: One provenance-stamp record. Every workspace-graph node, edge, impact-reason slot, and explainer-citation slot resolves to exactly one stamp record so later surfaces render 'why this is here, who said so, when, and through which extraction or inference path' from one vocabulary.

audit_event_id.description: Optional audit-event id support bundles, replay captures, and AI evidence packets cite verbatim.

source_anchor_drift_record.description: One stand-alone source-anchor-drift record. Used when a surface emits a drift event independent of (or in addition to) a parent provenance stamp — for example, when the anchor's drift_state changes between graph snapshots and the surface needs an auditable, replay-friendly drift event.
